title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's core expertise in AI-powered search, discovery, and personalization maps almost perfectly onto Airbnb's homepage/discovery mandate — hybrid search, ranking, behavioral personalization, A/B testing at scale, and conversational AI are all directly applicable. The Samsung.com role (10M+ monthly users, hybrid AI search across 3 international marketplaces, recommendation API, category finders) reads like a direct analog to what Airbnb needs. The one genuine gap is consumer travel/marketplace context and the "product narrative/marketing" expectation — Riley's background skews engineering-partner-heavy rather than externally facing storytelling — but this is manageable with tailoring.
recommendation_reason: Domain match is exceptionally strong — AI-powered discovery, personalization, and search at consumer scale are Riley's exact wheelhouse, and Airbnb's 8+ year bar is comfortably met with 9 years of experience.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping search, discovery, and personalization experiences at consumer scale — including hybrid AI search across international marketplaces and recommendation systems on platforms serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record of driving measurable growth (CTR +20–45%, AOV +25%, NPS +20%) by combining AI/ML expertise with sharp product intuition, rigorous experimentation, and cross-functional leadership across engineering, data science, design, and marketing teams.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy — increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% (from 30% to 45%) and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows.
gap_handling: The JD explicitly calls out "product messaging and delivering to customers and the media" — Riley's resume is light on this. Add a brief line in the cover letter or summary noting experience crafting executive-facing product narratives and cross-functional launch communications (the AEO pipeline and Samsung global expansion stories both involved exec alignment and launch storytelling — surface that angle).

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's search, discovery, and AI/ML background is a strong thematic match for this role — hybrid search, ranking optimization, behavioral signals, and relevance metrics are all directly represented in the Samsung experience. However, the role explicitly requires 7+ years in product at a "high-growth tech startup" focused on complex search systems, and emphasizes marketplace/wholesale B2B context (retailer-facing discovery) that differs from Riley's consumer e-commerce focus. The critical gap is seniority: this is a Staff PM role, which is one level above Riley's stated target (Senior/PM III), and Faire's hybrid in-office requirement (NYC or SF, 3 days/week) is a partial location mismatch for a Bellevue-based candidate.
recommendation_reason: Near-perfect domain and skill alignment on search algorithms, but the Staff PM seniority is a stretch, the B2B wholesale marketplace context is a gap, and in-person NYC/SF hybrid work conflicts with Riley's Bellevue base.
suggested_summary: AI Search Product Manager with 9+ years shipping ML-powered search, ranking, and discovery systems at scale — including hybrid search across international marketplaces at Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users) and ML-driven catalog tooling for 2M+ B2B sellers at Amazon. Proven track record translating complex algorithmic improvements into measurable business outcomes through rigorous experimentation, relevance metric design, and cross-functional leadership with data science and engineering teams.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ
gap_handling: The B2B wholesale/marketplace context (retailers buying from brands) is meaningfully different from consumer e-commerce. Riley should bridge this gap explicitly in the cover letter by framing the Amazon B2B seller experience as evidence of understanding two-sided marketplace dynamics, and emphasizing that search relevance challenges — query understanding, ranking tradeoffs, offline/online metric design — are domain-transferable. Acknowledge the Staff-level stretch directly by highlighting scope: 10M+ monthly users, multi-market rollout, and CEO-level executive alignment at Samsung are signals of Staff-caliber impact.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, strong A/B testing and funnel optimization chops, and deep e-commerce growth experience make her a credible candidate for a growth-focused PM role at an AI-first company. However, this role is explicitly centered on developer platform / API growth — onboarding, console, docs, billing, self-serve upgrade paths for technical builders — and Riley has no direct experience owning a developer platform or API product. Her Amazon stint is the closest proxy (B2B seller tools, ML pipelines), but it was not a developer-facing API growth role. The domain alignment on AI and growth is excellent; the PM archetype gap on "developer platform growth" is the key risk.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI and growth foundations, but the role demands developer platform / API growth experience Riley doesn't clearly demonstrate — heavy tailoring required to make the case.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth across AI-powered search, discovery, and e-commerce platforms, with deep fluency in funnel optimization, A/B experimentation, and cross-functional execution at scale. Passionate about building AI products that create real-world impact — eager to bring a track record of shipping intelligent, data-driven experiences to accelerating developer adoption and monetization on the Claude Platform.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — demonstrating experience owning technical, usage-based B2B product workflows end-to-end.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with engineering and data science to drive a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% through iterative A/B testing — showing structured growth experimentation across acquisition and monetization.
gap_handling: Riley has no direct experience owning a developer-facing API platform or technical growth funnel (onboarding, console, docs, billing). To address this gap, she should: (1) explicitly call out the Amazon ML listing tool as a technical, API-adjacent B2B product; (2) highlight any direct exposure to API integrations, webhook-based workflows, or developer tooling in her Samsung or Amazon work; (3) consider briefly noting her MS in Business Analytics and technical SQL/BigQuery depth as signals of technical credibility with engineering teams. If she has any side experience with developer tools, APIs, or has shipped anything with external API contracts, that should be surfaced prominently.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background — particularly the conversational AI chat experience, AEO pipeline, and hybrid search system at Samsung — maps well to Twilio's Copilot backend vision around LLMs, RAG, and intelligent agent orchestration. The domain alignment is strong on the AI and conversational AI dimensions, and several resume bullets directly demonstrate the contextual personalization and behavioral signal work Twilio requires. However, this role is titled "Principal PM," which sits above Riley's target seniority range, and it leans heavily into backend platform/infrastructure PM work (API orchestration, RAG pipelines, LLM latency/cost management, developer tooling) — a B2B platform archetype that is secondary to Riley's core consumer/e-commerce AI identity. The developer/API-first context at Twilio is also a meaningful gap vs. Riley's consumer-facing and e-commerce background.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM alignment and evidence of conversational AI and RAG-adjacent work, but the Principal-level seniority and backend platform/developer-tools focus are meaningful stretches from Riley's target profile.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences at scale — including a production on-site Copilot-style chat, hybrid search orchestration, and an AEO pipeline that drove 8x AI agent traffic. Proven ability to partner with ML engineers and data scientists to build contextual, personalization-driven AI systems that reduce friction and improve conversion across millions of users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for users across Samsung.com

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is the backend platform / developer-tools PM archetype: Twilio's customers are developers and the role centers on API orchestration, LLM infrastructure costs, and RAG pipelines — not consumer UX. Riley should explicitly call out any experience with API design, backend instrumentation strategy, or B2B/developer-facing products (the Amazon seller tool is the strongest bridge here). Also worth noting: the role is Principal PM, one level above Riley's stretch target — framing the application around scope of impact (10M+ users, global expansion leadership, cross-org alignment at Samsung) rather than title will be important.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background in AI-powered search, personalization, LLM pipelines, and experimentation maps well to the technical and domain demands of this role — especially the AEO pipeline, hybrid search, and recommendation API work at Samsung. However, this is explicitly a Staff PM role focused on platform infrastructure (retrieval systems, RAG, contextual data schemas, prompt optimization at scale) for customer support AI, which is a meaningful pivot from Riley's e-commerce discovery archetype. The role also targets 9+ years at Staff level — Riley is at the boundary of seniority but lacks explicit platform/infrastructure ownership and customer support domain experience, making this a real stretch.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/personalization signal and experimentation depth, but Staff-level platform infrastructure scope and customer support domain are meaningful gaps vs. Riley's e-commerce discovery background.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years building personalization and LLM-powered experiences at consumer scale, including hybrid search, recommendation APIs, and conversational AI for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com. Proven track record designing retrieval pipelines, defining experimentation frameworks, and partnering with ML engineers to ship foundational AI systems that drive measurable engagement and conversion outcomes.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Riley lacks explicit platform PM experience (owning shared infrastructure consumed by multiple surfaces) and has no customer support or community operations domain background — both central to this role. In the cover letter and resume summary, lean into the AEO pipeline and recommendation API as the closest analogues to "platform" ownership, and frame the conversational AI chat experience at Samsung as directly relevant to AI-powered support. Acknowledge the CX pivot honestly if asked, and position e-commerce discovery as a user-empathy-heavy domain that translates well to support personalization.

However, the role is firmly centered on customer support/help center experiences and CustOps enablement, which is not a domain Riley has direct experience in. The NYC hybrid requirement is also a geographic stretch from Bellevue, WA, though NYC is listed as a preferred location. The growth and discovery dimensions are well-matched, but the support-ops-specific territory (deflection rates, knowledge bases, agent tooling) is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI, discovery, and growth credentials are compelling, but no direct help-center or CustOps support product experience creates a real gap in a role where that's the core domain.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years specializing in discovery-led product experiences, conversational AI, and personalization at scale—including a launched on-site AI chat experience serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com. top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows
gap_handling: Riley has no direct help-center or CustOps enablement experience. title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, SQL/Tableau tooling, and experience evangelizing AI-driven products align well with Lyft's AI Platform & Marketplace role. However, the role is explicitly Staff-level (7+ years, CXO presentations, agentic/multi-agent orchestration, platform-as-product) — one level above Riley's target seniority — and is heavily focused on internal AI platform management and marketplace economics (ride-sharing), domains Riley has not directly worked in. The agentic systems / multi-agent orchestration requirement is a meaningful technical gap that Riley can only partially cover with conversational AI and AEO pipeline experience.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI PM fundamentals and tooling match, but Staff-level seniority stretch + agentic/multi-agent gap + marketplace/transportation domain mismatch make this competitive without significant tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI/ML-powered platforms — from hybrid search and ranking systems to conversational AI and LLM pipelines — at Samsung and Amazon, serving 10M+ users and 2M+ B2B sellers. Proven track record defining roadmaps, evangelizing AI adoption across cross-functional stakeholders, and delivering measurable business outcomes through rigorous experimentation and data-driven decision-making.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, leveraging behavioral insights to increase search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, a proven track record shipping search, discovery, and conversational AI at scale, and 9 years of PM experience that comfortably clears the 8-year bar. However, the role explicitly calls for a "former consumer founder" who spikes on 0-to-1 product creation — Riley's background is primarily 1-to-N optimization and feature scaling at established companies (Samsung, Amazon, Kolon), not greenfield consumer product invention or startup founding. The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping consumer-facing AI experiences — including conversational AI chat, hybrid search, and personalized discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users — with a track record of translating emerging model capabilities into products people love. Deeply hands-on across the full product lifecycle from vision and prototyping through scaled global launch, with a bias for creative, low-cost experimentation to validate product-market fit fast.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for millions of users on Samsung.com

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — a 0-to-1 initiative taken from concept to production

Launched hybrid AI-powered search experience across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: Riley has no stated startup founding experience — the single biggest filter for this role. To address this, the cover letter should reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI chat launches as 0-to-1 entrepreneurial bets driven by Riley's initiative, not top-down roadmap items. If Riley has any side projects, independent AI product experiments, or early-stage advisory work, those should be surfaced prominently. Leaning into hands-on prototyping with Claude or other AI tools (per the JD's "Claude Code" signal) would also help close the founder-proxy gap.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials and proven A/B testing, funnel optimization, and growth experience — but the role is explicitly a Growth/Monetization PM focused on subscription pricing, packaging, and free-to-paid conversion, which is not Riley's primary archetype. While Riley has touched AOV uplift, retention, and onboarding conversion at Kolon, there is no direct experience owning a subscription monetization funnel, pricing strategy, or upgrade flow at scale. The domain (AI consumer/platform products) is an excellent fit, and Anthropic is a top-tier AI brand, but the monetization specialization creates a meaningful gap versus candidates who have owned SaaS/subscription pricing as a core PM role.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain and growth signal, but Riley lacks direct ownership of subscription monetization, pricing/packaging, and paid conversion funnels — the core of this role.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years of experience driving growth and revenue impact across e-commerce, search, and AI-powered products — including recommendation systems that lifted AOV 25% on a $2B+ GMV platform and onboarding redesigns that boosted first-purchase rates 30%. Passionate about building ethical, user-centric AI products and experienced partnering with data scientists and engineers to run rigorous A/B experiments that translate into measurable business outcomes.
top_bullets: Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%; designed segmentation-driven CRM program boosting 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The core gap is direct ownership of subscription monetization mechanics — pricing tiers, packaging, and paid conversion funnels. To address this, Riley should explicitly frame the Kolon onboarding/retention work as conversion funnel ownership, quantify any revenue impact from the conversational AI and recommendation work at Samsung (e.g., conversion +40% from AEO pipeline), and consider adding a brief cover letter paragraph acknowledging the shift from discovery/search PM to monetization PM — positioning it as a deliberate move into growth-focused roles at an AI-first company.

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, ranking systems, and experimentation — that partially translate to this role. However, the core requirement is deep BI/analytics domain expertise (semantic layers, data warehouses, governance consoles, modern data stack), which is largely absent from Riley's resume. The role is also firmly B2B SaaS-first and 0-to-1 in a new product line, whereas Riley's background is predominantly consumer/e-commerce discovery. The SQL skill and AI/LLM fluency are genuine overlaps, but the data infrastructure and analytics-specific domain knowledge represent a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM and experimentation credentials partially bridge the gap, but missing BI/data stack domain depth makes this a competitive stretch requiring significant resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, query understanding systems, and AI-driven data products at Samsung and Amazon, serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record designing eval frameworks, governing ML model outputs, and translating complex data signals into trustworthy, high-engagement product experiences for technical and non-technical stakeholders alike.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: Riley lacks explicit BI/analytics product experience (Tableau, Looker, warehouse-native tools, semantic layers). In the resume and cover letter, lean into the AEO structured-data pipeline and the CDP work at Kolon as the closest analogs to governance and data curation. Acknowledge the observability-meets-analytics angle by connecting search instrumentation and behavioral data platform work to the "data consumer" persona Datadog is targeting. Be prepared for interview questions on dbt, Snowflake/BigQuery semantic layers, and NLP-to-SQL systems — worth doing targeted prep.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

title: Principal Product Manager, AI & Automation
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product shipping credentials — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI, AEO — directly satisfy Okta's "2+ years shipping AI/ML products in production" bar, and the multi-cross-functional leadership style maps well to the role's IT/HR/Finance/GTM partnership expectations. However, the role is titled "Principal PM," which sits one level above Riley's target of Senior/Staff PM, and requires 10+ years of PM experience where Riley has ~9. The most significant gap is domain: Okta's context is enterprise identity/security SaaS (B2B platform), a departure from Riley's consumer e-commerce and search background — the AI work here centers on agents, copilots, and workflow automation for internal enterprise use cases, not discovery or ranking.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI shipping credentials offset a meaningful domain gap, but Principal-level title and enterprise identity/security context make this a real stretch — worth applying with heavy tailoring toward AI evaluation, 0→1 innovation, and agentic systems.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-native experiences — from LLM-driven pipelines and conversational AI to hybrid search and recommendation systems — with a track record of taking products from 0→1 innovation to scaled adoption across 10M+ users. Proven ability to define AI quality metrics, build cross-functional alignment across complex organizations, and deliver measurable business impact at enterprise scale.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Lean into the agentic/LLM side of your AEO pipeline and conversational AI work.

title: Product Manager, Claude Code
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background and proven track record shipping intelligent systems at scale (Samsung search, Amazon ML pipelines) are genuinely relevant to this AI-first role at Anthropic. However, Claude Code is squarely a developer tools / CLI product, and Riley has zero explicit dev tools, CLI, or developer-facing product experience — a hard requirement that dominates the job description. The role also explicitly requires at least 1 year as a professional software engineer, which is not evidenced anywhere in Riley's resume; this is a structural gap that will screen out many otherwise-strong candidates. The brand, compensation ($285–305K), and visa sponsorship are all highly favorable.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML product credentials and sponsored visa are great fits, but missing engineer background and zero dev tools experience are hard gaps the JD explicitly calls out.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping intelligent systems — including LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and agentic workflows — at Samsung and Amazon. Passionate about building developer-facing AI experiences that translate cutting-edge model capabilities into practical, high-adoption tools at scale.
top_bullets: Defined automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — demonstrating hands-on LLM pipeline and agentic workflow ownership.

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system with multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80% — closest analog to technical, developer-adjacent tooling.

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — showing ability to ship complex AI product experiences cross-functionally.
gap_handling: The "1+ year as a professional engineer" requirement is explicit and structural — address it head-on in a cover letter by highlighting any hands-on technical work (SQL, BigQuery, prompt engineering, instrumentation strategy) and your deep collaboration pattern with ML/engineering teams. If you have any coding background (coursework, side projects, early-career work) not currently on the resume, now is the time to surface it. Also explicitly call out your personal use of AI coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, or Copilot to demonstrate the "hands-on with AI coding tools" signal the JD demands.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic workflows, and multi-agent signals — that are directionally relevant. However, this role is squarely in enterprise identity security (IAM, OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust), a domain Riley has no documented experience in, which is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have. Additionally, the role is developer-platform / B2B enterprise, a departure from Riley's consumer/e-commerce core. The LLM and AI agent awareness partially bridges the gap, but the identity infrastructure expertise is a meaningful missing piece.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product background is genuinely relevant, but the IAM/OAuth/OIDC domain expertise is a hard requirement Riley can't currently satisfy — needs honest gap acknowledgment.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and multi-agent workflows at Samsung and Amazon, reaching 10M+ monthly users. Passionate about the next frontier of AI agent identity and governance, bringing a deep track record of cross-functional technical collaboration, data-driven experimentation, and zero-to-one product delivery.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting AI-driven product discovery for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system using multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is IAM domain knowledge (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust). Before applying, Riley should invest a few hours in self-study — read Okta's developer docs on OAuth2/OIDC flows, skim the emerging MCP (Model Context Protocol) spec, and reference any API design or token-based auth work (even indirectly) in the resume. In the cover letter, frame the AEO pipeline and multi-agent workflow familiarity as a direct bridge to AI agent governance, and be upfront that the identity security domain is a learning edge — Okta may value the fresh AI product instincts over a traditional IAM background for this zero-to-one charter.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience
score_rationale: Riley's core PM skills — roadmapping, cross-functional leadership, A/B testing, and data-driven execution — are solid matches for Asana's expectations at the Senior PM level, and the 5+ year requirement is well exceeded. However, the role is squarely B2B enterprise SaaS / work management (task & project collaboration tooling), a domain Riley has minimal direct exposure to — her background is almost entirely e-commerce, search, and AI discovery. The preferred "complex data models, visualizations, and human-centric UX" signals a UX-heavy, systems-design PM archetype that doesn't align closely with Riley's AI/ML search and growth archetypes. There are no strong resume bullets that directly prove enterprise SaaS feature shipping, task/project UX, or B2B SaaS platform experience.
recommendation_reason: Solid PM fundamentals and seniority match, but domain and archetype misalignment (enterprise SaaS / work management vs. AI search / e-commerce) make this a stretch requiring significant resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building data-driven, user-centric product experiences at scale — including B2B platform tooling at Amazon serving 2M+ sellers and AI-powered discovery systems for 10M+ monthly users. Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams across engineering, design, and data science to ship high-velocity roadmaps that drive measurable business impact.
top_bullets: Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for B2B sellers that reduced manual contacts with support agents — partnered with 5 dependent teams to improve listing experience based on 10+ interviews and 300+ seller surveys.

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering on multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels — increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.
gap_handling: Riley's B2B work at Amazon is her strongest bridge to this role — lean hard into the seller-tools platform experience, systems complexity, and cross-team coordination. Acknowledge in a cover letter that this is a domain pivot, but frame AI curiosity and data-model fluency (BigQuery, behavioral signals, catalog pipelines) as directly applicable to Asana's enterprise data model challenges.

title: Senior Product Manager, APM - Observability
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths are in AI-powered search, e-commerce discovery, and personalization — domains that are meaningfully different from the APM/Observability/distributed tracing focus this role demands. While Elastic is a "Search AI Company" and Riley has strong search credentials, this role is squarely in DevOps/developer tooling (APM, Synthetics, RUM, OpenTelemetry) — a vertical with no evidence in Riley's resume. The seniority level and remote-US setup are good fits, but the domain gap is the dominant factor pulling the score down.
recommendation_reason: Hard domain mismatch — APM, distributed tracing, and observability tooling are not represented anywhere in Riley's background, making this a stretch that would require significant re-skilling narrative.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered data pipelines, structured metadata systems, and instrumentation strategies at scale — including behavioral signal instrumentation and search telemetry at Samsung.com for 10M+ monthly users. Bringing a customer-backwards product methodology and cross-functional execution track record to complex, data-rich developer-facing product domains.
top_bullets: Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, leveraging behavioral insights to iterate search experiences — increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system with multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: If Riley wants to pursue this role, the cover letter must directly acknowledge the domain shift and frame transferable signals: instrumentation strategy, telemetry pipeline ownership, and structured data work at Samsung/Amazon are the closest bridges. Riley would also benefit from demonstrating any hands-on familiarity with OpenTelemetry, Elastic Stack, or developer tooling in a side project or self-study — without that, the application will likely be screened out early by recruiters filtering for APM experience.
